steps.title: Write the perimeter formula
steps.content: Perimeter of a rectangle: $P = 2L + 2W$. With length $45$ and width $x$: $P = 2(45) + 2x = 90 + 2x$.

steps.title: Translate the constraint
steps.content:
“At most 190 inches” means the perimeter is less than or equal to 190: $2x + 90 \le 190$.
  • A ($2x + 90 \le 190$): Matches both the perimeter expression and the ≤ constraint. A is correct.
  • B ($2x + 90 \ge 190$): Uses $\ge$, meaning “at least” — wrong direction.
  • C ($2x + 45 \le 190$): Constant should be $2 \times 45 = 90$, not 45 — wrong coefficient.
  • D ($2x + 45 \ge 190$): Both the constant and the inequality direction are wrong.
